result.summary: Bandwidth exposes a broad authenticated HTTP API suite, a local MCP server package for AI agents, and an authenticated `band` CLI.
result.description: Bandwidth is a cloud communications platform for voice calling, text messaging, phone numbers, emergency services, and related telecom workflows. Its products include APIs and tools for provisioning, messaging, voice, account management, and AI-agent integrations.

bw_oauth_client.label: Bandwidth API Credential (client ID and client secret)
bw_oauth_client.setup: In [The Bandwidth App](https://app.bandwidth.com/), go to **Account → API Credentials** and click **Create**. Fill in description, account scope, roles, and accounts, then save. Store the `clientSecret` when shown; it is only displayed once. Your `clientId`/`clientSecret` are then used to obtain OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens via the client-credentials flow.

bw_api_user.label: Bandwidth legacy API user (username and password)
bw_api_user.setup: Legacy Bandwidth API Users are deprecated. The credential-management docs state creation of new API Users is disabled as of April 1, 2026, updates are disabled July 1, 2026, and they are fully decommissioned December 2, 2026. If you still have an existing legacy API user, its `username` and `password` can be used where legacy auth is still supported during the migration period. See the [credential management guide](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management) and [migration guide](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/migration/).

packages.runtimeHint: Python/uvx local stdio server from GitHub source
